no silent constraint or contact omission

limitations: The selected contact default changes VBD from hard AL/C0 to penalty-only contact; it is Track B relative to the old hard-contact baseline.
The hard-contact alternative passes 5/5 but uses 94.64% of the penetration gate in the worst repeat.
Neither dense policy is bitwise repeatable; all ten robustness runs nevertheless pass the frozen gates.
The coupled global KKT prototype is accurate on 1/8/64 controlled contacts but stalls at first high-friction pile impact and is not promoted.
Smooth broad rows are candidate audits; only the five canonical Y rows are matched speed comparisons.
This is a measured dispatcher, not a universal solver or convergence theorem.
verdict: MEASURED CABLE PORTFOLIO: SMOOTH-GLOBAL WIN + FIRST DENSE-CONTACT PASSES
